recalls.Description: Gagaku has issued a recall for their ice cream shop toy playsets, which include 16 wooden pieces such as a cash register, ice cream cones, scoops, popsicles, and cupcakes. The set also features a plastic credit card, dollar bills, a menu, and a two-level display box labeled "ICE-CREAM." The product is identified by the label "GAGAKU" and model number "KABI-0176 G062401IC" on the underside of the toy. These toys pose a serious ingestion hazard due to high-powered magnets that can detach and be swallowed by children, leading to severe health risks including intestinal blockage and potential death.
recalls.Title: Gagaku Ice Cream Toy Playsets Recalled Due to Magnet Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Brandworl.USA via email at brandworl@gmail.com.

Products.Name: Gagaku Ice Cream Shop Toy Playsets

Images.Caption: Recalled Gagaku Wooden Ice Cream Shop Toy Playsets
"GAGAKU" and "Model: KABI-0176 G062401IC" are printed on a label on the underside of the toy

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from October 2024 through June 2025, priced between $17 and $30.

Distributors.Name: Brandworl Management Co., Limited, dba Brandworl.USA, of Hong Kong

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys, keep them away from children, and contact Brandworl.USA for a full refund. Destroy the toy by marking each piece "RECALLED" and send a photo of the destroyed toy to Brandworl@gmail.com before disposing of it.

recalls.metaDescription: Gagaku recalls ice cream toy playsets due to a serious ingestion risk from magnets. Learn how to get a refund and safely dispose of the toys.

recalls.Description: Backyard Kids has issued a recall for the KidKraft Farm to Table play kitchen, designed for children aged 3 and older. This play kitchen features a black and light gray design with wood-like countertops, measuring approximately 44 inches in length, 13 ¾ inches in width, and 43 inches in height. The model number 53411 can be found on a sticker located on the back of the unit.
recalls.Title: Backyard Kids Recalls KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchens Due to Strangulation Risk; One Fatal Incident Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers should reach out to Backyard Kids at 800-882-0234, available Monday through Friday from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at recall@kidkraft.com. More details can also be found online at www.kidkraft.com/pages/recall.

Products.Name: KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchen
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 192,000 (In addition, 12 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchen
Recalled KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchen (the hooks are circled)

Injuries.Name: In February 2023 in Oregon, a 23-month-old child was asphyxiated after his shirt got caught on one of the hooks while climbing and crawling through the opening at the back of the play kitchen where the toy kitchen accessories are held. The child was unable to rescue himself and died from strangulation and asphyxia.

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at KidKraft.com, Amazon.com, and Walmart.com from 2018 until July 2025, priced between $120 and $270.

Distributors.Name: Backyard Kids, LLC, of Plano, Texas

Hazards.Name: Strangulation hazard due to clothing getting caught on hooks

Remedies.Name: Free replacement hooks provided by Backyard Kids

recalls.metaDescription: Backyard Kids recalls KidKraft Farm to Table play kitchens due to a strangulation hazard, following one reported death. Learn more here.

recalls.Description: The recall affects Xylolin peg doll sets featuring nine colorful peg dolls and a natural wood base designed to hold them. The product, marked with "Model SD13" on its packaging, poses a serious choking risk due to the spherical ends of the dolls, which can obstruct a child's airway. This hazard could lead to severe injury or even death.
recalls.Title: Xylolin Peg Doll Toy Sets Recalled Due to Choking Hazard and Federal Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Babywants via email at recall@twoodtoy.com or visit their recall page at www.xylolintoys.com/pages/recall-page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Xylolin peg dolls and pedestal toy set
Recalled Xylolin peg dolls with pedestal toy set - packaging
"Model SD13" is printed on a white label located on the side of the product packaging

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from May 2021 until January 2025 for approximately $20.

Importers.Name: Taizhoushi Ermu Jinchukou Co., Ltd., dba Babywants, and owner of the Xylolin brand, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to peg dolls' spherical ends

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product, dispose of it, and send a photo to Babywants for a full refund. Amazon will contact all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Xylolin Peg Doll Sets recalled due to choking hazards. Immediate action required for safety. Contact Babywants for refunds.

recalls.Description:
MGA Entertainment is recalling its Miniverse Make It Mini Sets that contain unused liquid resins, which pose risks of skin, eye, and respiratory irritation. The recalled sets include various models such as Make It Mini Appliances, Make It Mini Food, and Make It Mini Lifestyle. The resins can imitate food items and may cause harm if inhaled, touched, or ingested. Once the resins cure, they no longer present these hazards.

Consumers are advised to stop using any sets with unused resins immediately. The packaging features a yellow burst background with the brand name and product details. Affected items can be identified by their item numbers and UPC codes printed on the wrappers. The products were sold individually in spheres or bundled in boxes at various retailers across the country from October 2022 to June 2024.
recalls.Title: MGA Entertainment Issues Recall for Miniverse Make It Mini Sets Due to Potential Skin, Eye, and Respiratory Irritation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact MGA Entertainment at 800-222-4685 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or 9 a.m. to 2 p.m. ET Saturday. You can also email mvcustomer_care@mgae.com or visit https://www.mgae.com/customer-care/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Miniverse Make It Mini Sets with Liquid Resins
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 21 million (In addition, about 1 million in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – Food
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – Appliances
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini - Lifestyle
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – Kitchen
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – All You Can Eat
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini Appliances
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini Lifestyle
Recalled Make It Mini Food – 18 Pack
Recalled Make It Mini Food Ice Cream Social - Front
Recalled Make It Mini Food Ice Cream Social - Opened

Injuries.Name: MGA has received 26 reports of incidents with this product from children and adults, including reports of skin burns and irritation, and respiratory irritation, with one report that a consumer's asthma was triggered.

Retailers.Name: Target, Walmart, Family Dollar, Dollar General, ALDI, Hobby Lobby, and various other stores nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, shop.mgae.com, Target.com, and Walmart.com.

Hazards.Name: The recalled Make It Mini sets contain resins that can cause skin, eye, and respiratory irritation or sensitization when inhaled, touched, or ingested by children or adults.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using any units with unused resins and contact MGA to obtain a pre-paid label for returns, either for the entire unopened product or the unused resins along with a photo of the opened product.

recalls.metaDescription: MGA recalls Miniverse Make It Mini Sets due to risks of skin, eye, and respiratory irritation. Stop using affected products immediately.

recalls.Description: This recall affects several dress-up playsets from Litti City and Litti Pritti, along with Prextex slime eggs. The Litti City premier doctor playset includes a white doctor outfit with accessories like blue glasses and a yellow stethoscope. The Litti City teacher purse playset features a green purse packed with educational tools. Additionally, the Litti Pritti handbag playset comes with a pink purse and various accessories. The Prextex slime eggs are clear plastic containers filled with colorful slime and mini bottles of shaped beads.
recalls.Title: Recall of Litti City and Litti Pritti Dress-Up Playsets and Prextex Slime Eggs Due to Toxic Phthalates and Lead Levels
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled items immediately and reach out to Perch at 888-359-4676, available from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT Monday through Friday. They can also email playsets@realtimeresults.net or visit www.recallrtr.com/playsets for further details on disposal and obtaining a full refund.

Images.Caption: Recalled Litti City Premier Doctor Playset
Recalled Litti Pritti Handbag Playset
Recalled Litti City Teacher Purse Set
Recalled Prextex DIY Take-Along Slime Eggs

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com; the Litti Pritti handbag playset was also sold at Ocean State Job Lot stores in the northeast from July 2021 through April 2023, priced between $9 and $45.

Importers.Name: Whele LLC d/b/a Perch, of Boston, Massachusetts
Amusty Inc. of Staten Island, New York
Bingo Deals of Harriman, New York

Hazards.Name: Excessive levels of phthalates and lead in dress-up playsets and slime eggs pose health risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled products and contact Perch for disposal instructions and a refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Litti City and Litti Pritti playsets and Prextex slime eggs due to harmful phthalates and lead levels. Immediate action required.
